title: Political socialization
description: Explain how cultural factors influence political attitudes and socialization

assessment_items.question_content:
**Political socialization is best defined as which of the following? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The process by which people form their ideas about politics and acquire their ideas about government
A citizen’s belief that their vote matters and can influence government policies
A collection of citizens' opinions on politics and the role of government
The process of redrawing the geographic boundaries of legislative districts

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of the process of political socialization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Parents discuss at the dinner table who they are voting for in the next presidential election
A teacher gives a student a detention for punching another student
A nine year old girl watches a cartoon episode about superheroes
Two friends decide to make a “boys-only” clubhouse in the forest behind their backyards

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios is an example of the process of political socialization in schools?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Students are required to take an American government course to graduate from high school
A teacher refuses to discuss who he is voting for in the next presidential election
An English class engages in a heated discussion about Shakespeare's *A Midsummer's Night Dream*
The basketball team uses a paper vote system to decide who should be captain

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following is NOT an agent of political socialization? **

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Sports teams
Family
Schools
Media

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following agents of socialization is the most successful at transmitting political ideologies?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Family
Peers
Media
Religious organizations

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![Bar graph show the percentage of religious service attendees who have heard religious leaders speak about political or social issues. The X-axis show percentage and the Y-axis lists different political and social issues. Forty percent of religious service attendees have heard their religious leader speak about religious liberty. Thirty-nine percent have heard their religious leaders speak about homosexuality. Twenty-nine percent have heard their religious leader speak about abortion. Twenty-seven percent have heard their religious leader speak about immigration. Twenty-two percent have heard their religious leader speak about environmental issues. Eighteen percent have heard their religious leader speak about economic inequality. Sixty-four percent have heard their religious leader speak about speak one about at least one of these issues. ](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/2f2cf568202b7d4cacb01abc4fc6e6f9.png)

> Data from [Pew Research Center](http://www.pewforum.org/2016/08/08/many-americans-hear-politics-from-the-pulpit/)

**A political scientist might use this data to support which of the following arguments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Religious organizations are major agents of political socialization because religious leaders speak about social or political issues
Religious organizations are not agents of political socialization because they do not speak about social or political issues at a rate significant enough to affect attitudes
Religious organizations are major agents of political socialization because religious leaders refuse to speak about social or political issues
Religious organizations are not agents of political socialization because only church members speak about social or political issues, not religious leaders

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the image below to answer the following question. 

![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/275385f4f5bb4fbdfd2de371ce49cfb6.png)

> Data from [Pew Research Center](http://www.pewforum.org/fact-sheet/changing-attitudes-on-gay-marriage/)

**Which of the following is an accurate conclusion based on a comparison of the trends in the line graph above and your knowledge of political socialization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: A person’s attitude about same-sex marriage can depend on which religious organization they take part in
A person’s attitude about same-sex marriage has nothing to do with their religious preferences
Religious people are more likely to vote Republican than unaffiliated people because they heavily support same-sex marriage
Religious people are more likely to vote Democrat than unaffiliated people because they heavily support same-sex marriage

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best reflects the process of political socialization?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The Belchers, a family of five, discuss the platforms of the 2020 presidential candidates.
Representative Pescadero always decides whether he will support legislation based on his constituents' wishes.
Senator Frond and Senator Ambrose promise to trade votes to benefit each other’s pieces of legislation.
Teddy donates several thousand dollars to support Representative Lenny DeStefano’s campaign.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Paid family leave won’t only make life easier for Moms and Dads – it will also keep skilled, talented Americans in the workforce and grow our economy. That’s why every other advanced country already has it.”

>Hillary Clinton in a speech in Warren, Michigan, source: [Newsweek](http://www.newsweek.com/hillary-clinton-full-transcript-economic-speech-489602)


**This quote is an example of which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: The values of other countries have influenced political attitudes in the United States
The values of the United States have influenced political attitudes in other countries
The values of the United States have remained unchanged as the rest of the world has participated in globalization
The values of the United States have had no influence on the political attitudes of other countries

assessment_items.question_content:
> “There's something even more revolutionary at work here. By lowering the barriers that protect state-owned industries, China is speeding a process that is removing government from vast areas of people's lives. In the past, virtually every Chinese citizen woke up in an apartment or a house owned by the government, went to work in a factory or a farm run by the government and read newspapers published by the government. . . .

> “Now people are leaving those firms, and when China joins the W.T.O., they will leave them faster. 

> “The Chinese government no longer will be everyone's employer, landlord, shopkeeper and nanny all rolled into one. It will have fewer instruments, therefore, with which to control people's lives. And that may lead to very profound change. . . . The genie of freedom will not go back into the bottle. As Justice Earl Warren once said, “liberty is the most contagious force in the world.” 

>In the new century, liberty will spread by cell phone and cable modem. In the past year, the number of Internet addresses in China has more than quadrupled, from two million to nine million. This year, the number is expected to grow to over 20 million.”

>-Source: President Bill Clinton, “Clinton’s Words on China: Trade Is the Smart Thing,” *The New York Times,* 2000

**Which of the following statements best summarizes Clinton’s argument?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: If China becomes more involved in international organizations, its citizens will adopt aspects of American political culture.
If the relationship between the United States and China strengthens, communism will spread to the United States.
If China engages in international trade, its economic power will weaken over time, leading to US dominance.
If China joins international trade organizations, the US economy will improve because it can sell more technology to China.

assessment_items.question_content:
> “There's something even more revolutionary at work here. By lowering the barriers that protect state-owned industries, China is speeding a process that is removing government from vast areas of people's lives. In the past, virtually every Chinese citizen woke up in an apartment or a house owned by the government, went to work in a factory or a farm run by the government and read newspapers published by the government. . . .

> “Now people are leaving those firms, and when China joins the W.T.O., they will leave them faster. 

> “The Chinese government no longer will be everyone's employer, landlord, shopkeeper and nanny all rolled into one. It will have fewer instruments, therefore, with which to control people's lives. And that may lead to very profound change. . . . The genie of freedom will not go back into the bottle. As Justice Earl Warren once said, “liberty is the most contagious force in the world.” 

>In the new century, liberty will spread by cell phone and cable modem. In the past year, the number of Internet addresses in China has more than quadrupled, from two million to nine million. This year, the number is expected to grow to over 20 million.”

>-Source: President Bill Clinton, “Clinton’s Words on China: Trade Is the Smart Thing,” *The New York Times,* 2000

**Which of the following statements is most consistent with Clinton’s argument in this passage?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Globalization can spread US political culture to countries around the world.
The United States needs to engage in free trade to be economically successful.
The president should be in charge of setting the foreign policy agenda.
Political beliefs are set at a young age and rarely change as people get older.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following scenarios best reflects the globalization of US political culture?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Following a political revolution and a strong trade relationship with the US, the country of Corto Maltese starts hosting free and open elections.
After a series of national disasters, the Bialyian government signs an international agreement to address the effects of climate change.
The country of Kahndaq signs a peace treaty with the United States after several years of military conflict.
The Markovian government shifts from a constitutional democracy to a monarchy after a regime change.
